title: tecoma fulva subsp. garrocha
intro: Tecoma fulva subsp. garrocha, also known as Garrocha, is a plant species that belongs to the Bignoniaceae family, specifically a type of flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. The medicinal properties of Tecoma fulva subsp. garrocha include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Tecoma fulva subsp. garrocha can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
